text: Fill in the blanks about gastrointestinal physiology.
questions: Gastric acid (HCl) is secreted by *parietal/oxyntic* cells, while pepsinogen is secreted by *chief/peptic* cells in the gastric glands.
The three phases of gastric secretion are: *cephalic* (vagal, before food reaches stomach), gastric (food in stomach), and *intestinal* (food in duodenum).
Bile is produced by the *liver* and stored in the *gallbladder*, and its main function is emulsification of fats.
The migrating motor complex (MMC) is the *interdigestive* motility pattern that sweeps residual food and bacteria from the small intestine, regulated by *motilin*.
Most nutrient absorption occurs in the *jejunum*, while bile salts and vitamin B12 are specifically absorbed in the *ileum*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
